Sample.Title: ctrl30 [NGS247_0001R_RFS28]

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: doesed with 5mM stokcs of metcamifen or cyprosulfamide, prepared in dimethyl fulfoxide (DMSO) to give a final concentration of 5uM, or DMSO alone as control treatmnet. Cells sampled at 30,90, and 240 minutes.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: Cell suspension cultures 5 day old; n=3
Channel.Extract-Protocol: Total RNA was extracted using RiboPureTM RNA Purification Kit (Ambion, Inc.) Illumina Kit

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequenced on Illumina HiSeqs 2000 system at 9 samples per lane; 100 bp SE reads FastQ files aligned to Oryza sativa cv japonica genome v7 nuclear and organelle genomes using GSNAP verion 2011_03_38. Count data were generated at gene level using annoations for nuclear, chloroplast and mitochondrial genomes using custom Python script; each gene received a count of +1 if FastQ file aligned in gene region definge in annotation (GFF) file for genome; single read counted multiple times: if aligned to up to 6 gene regions Futher processing for publication was done in R libraries EDAseq and EdgeR Genome_build: Oryza sativa cv japonica genome v7 nuclear and organelle genomes Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Data matrix of raw count data

Sample.Title: ctrl30 [NGS247_0002R_RFS29]

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: doesed with 5mM stokcs of metcamifen or cyprosulfamide, prepared in dimethyl fulfoxide (DMSO) to give a final concentration of 5uM, or DMSO alone as control treatmnet. Cells sampled at 30,90, and 240 minutes.

Channel.Extract-Protocol: Total RNA was extracted using RiboPureTM RNA Purification Kit (Ambion, Inc.) Illumina Kit

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequenced on Illumina HiSeqs 2000 system at 9 samples per lane; 100 bp SE reads FastQ files aligned to Oryza sativa cv japonica genome v7 nuclear and organelle genomes using GSNAP verion 2011_03_38. Count data were generated at gene level using annoations for nuclear, chloroplast and mitochondrial genomes using custom Python script; each gene received a count of +1 if FastQ file aligned in gene region definge in annotation (GFF) file for genome; single read counted multiple times: if aligned to up to 6 gene regions Futher processing for publication was done in R libraries EDAseq and EdgeR Genome_build: Oryza sativa cv japonica genome v7 nuclear and organelle genomes Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Data matrix of raw count data

Sample.Title: cypro240 [NGS247_0027R_RFS54]

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: doesed with 5mM stokcs of metcamifen or cyprosulfamide, prepared in dimethyl fulfoxide (DMSO) to give a final concentration of 5uM, or DMSO alone as control treatmnet. Cells sampled at 30,90, and 240 minutes.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: Cell suspension cultures 5 day old; n=3
Channel.Extract-Protocol: Total RNA was extracted using RiboPureTM RNA Purification Kit (Ambion, Inc.) Illumina Kit

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequenced on Illumina HiSeqs 2000 system at 9 samples per lane; 100 bp SE reads FastQ files aligned to Oryza sativa cv japonica genome v7 nuclear and organelle genomes using GSNAP verion 2011_03_38. Count data were generated at gene level using annoations for nuclear, chloroplast and mitochondrial genomes using custom Python script; each gene received a count of +1 if FastQ file aligned in gene region definge in annotation (GFF) file for genome; single read counted multiple times: if aligned to up to 6 gene regions Futher processing for publication was done in R libraries EDAseq and EdgeR Genome_build: Oryza sativa cv japonica genome v7 nuclear and organelle genomes Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Data matrix of raw count data

Series.Title: Transcriptome response to safener treatment in rice cell cultures
Series.Summary: Safeners are an important group of agrochemicals that can extend the selective use of existing herbicides into a wider range of cereals by protecting the crops from chemical injury. The recently released aromatic sulphonamide safeners cyprosulfamide and metcamifen were tested for their ability to protect rice plants from the phytotoxicity caused by clodinafop-propargyl, a herbicide normally only used in wheat. Transcriptome studies in rice cultures demonstrated that whereas cyprosulfamide had a negligible effect on gene expression over a 4 h exposure, metcamifen perturbed the abundance of 590 transcripts. Analysis of the inducible genes suggested that safening elicited similar gene families to those associated with specific biotic and abiotic stresses, notably those elicited by abscisic acid, salicylic acid and methyl-jasmonate.
Series.Overall-Design: Changes in the transcriptome of rice cultures exposed to metcamifen (5 µM) or cyprosulfamide (5 µM) were determined as compared with control cells treated with the carrier solvent DMSO. Cultures were sampled at 0.5 h, 1.5 h and 4 h after dosing and subjected to RNA-seq analysis. Three biological replicates were sequenced for each sample for a total of 27 samples
Series.Type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
